political.s: FEC: $285K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 11 executive donors.

charity.s: $16M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.05% of revenue).
charity.focus: Food security, youth/education, basic needs, community well-being

environment.s: hazardous waste violation: $2.7M in federal penalties (Violation Tracker).

guns.s: No federal firearms license on record for this company in the ATF FFL registry — no licensed firearms dealing or manufacturing.

execPay.s: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 911:1 (median employee pay $16,214) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

wiki.extract: Dollar Tree, Inc., formerly known as Dollar Tree Stores, Inc., is an American multi-price-point chain of discount variety stores. Headquartered in Chesapeake, Virginia, it is a Fortune 500 company and operates 15,115 stores throughout the 48 contiguous U.S. states and Canada. Its stores are supported by a nationwide logistics network of 24 distribution centers.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by rolling powered vehicle or machinery
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On May 23, 2025, an employee was training to drive an end-rider forklift when they lost balance and stepped off the forklift. Their left ankle was caught between the forklift and a pallet of water. The employee was hospitalized with injury to their ankle and required surgery.

An employee was on break in her car in the parking lot when an individual came out of a nearby store and began yelling at her. The employee was shot by this person three times in her torso/chest. The employee was hospitalized.
On 4/28/2025, an employee was working in the store and felt hot and dizzy due to heat exposure. The employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Hitting, kicking, beating by other person  n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On April 15, 2025, an employee was physically assaulted by an aggressive customer. The employee sustained broken and missing teeth as well as facial lacerations and was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught, entangled in running powered equipment  normal operation
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was crossing a conveyor belt when her left foot became caught on the conveyor belt. As the employee stepped down with her right foot, she was unable to move her left foot, resulting in a fractured left foot.

mostRecent.reason: CGMP Deviations: OTC products were stored outside of labeled temperature requirements.

The packaging is not child resistant as required by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The burn relief gel contains lidocaine, posing a poisoning risk if swa…

sampleRecalls.title: Dollar Tree Recalls More than One Million Hot Glue Guns Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Crafter's Square Glue Guns. They dispense hot glue when plugged into an outlet and the trigger is depressed. The plastic glue gun is black with an orange trigger and tip. The glue guns have a silver UL listed label located above the handle with "GLUE GUN" and "XY-15302" printed on the label.
hazards: The glue gun can malfunction when plugged in, posing fire and burn hazards.
injuries: Dollar Tree has received seven reports of electrical malfunctions when using the glue guns, including four reports of fire and one report of skin irritation.
units: About 1,025,000 (In addition, about 21,900 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: ADCO Recalls Candles Due to Fire and Burn Hazards; Sold Exclusively at Dollar Tree
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves the Sure Scents 2-1 Peaceful Stream/Moonlit Waves Candle. The candle is approximately 2½ inches tall with the powder blue color wax encased in a glass votive.
hazards: The candles' high flames can ignite the surface of the wax or cause the glass to break, posing fire and burn hazards.
injuries: The firm has received two reports that flame height reached above the glass, causing the glass to break. No injuries have been reported.
products: Sure Scents 2-1 Peaceful Stream/Moonlit Waves Candles

sampleRecalls.title: Dollar Tree Recalls Burn Relief Gel Due to Failure to Meet Child Resistant Closure Requirement
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled burn relief gel is packaged in a blue box with white letters "Burn Relief" and red letters "Burn Relief Gel". The brand name is Assured™. Inside the box is a blue and white tube labeled "Burn Relief Gel" and measuring approximately 5 inches long by 1 inch wide and weighs about 0.7 ounces (20 grams). The p…
hazards: The packaging is not child resistant as required by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The burn relief gel contains lidocaine, posing a poisoning risk if swa…

sampleRecalls.title: Dollar Tree Recalls Glass Votive Candle Holders Due to Fire and Laceration Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves frosted or clear glass votive candle holders with French vanilla scented candles. The votive candle holders are 2 1/2 inches tall. Model number 976127 and date code 1010 are printed on the bottom of the glass votive candle holder.
hazards: The glass votive candle holders can shatter while in use, posing a fire and laceration hazard to consumers.
injuries: The firm has received one report of the glass votive candle holder shattering. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Dollar Tree Recalls Children's Halloween Lanterns Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves plastic Halloween-themed lanterns designed to resemble a pumpkin, ghost and skull. The lanterns are about 6 1/2 inches tall and were sold in orange, white and black. Model number 954437-13096-003-1005 is printed on the bottom of the lanterns.
hazards: The bulb in the battery-operated lanterns can overheat, posing fire and burn hazards to consumers.
injuries: The firm has received one report of the bulb in a lantern overheating. No injuries have been reported.
products: Pumpkin, Ghost and Skull Halloween Lanterns
